columns.label: canonical reference
columns.what: The library that defines the ground truth for each method (sklearn for `pls`/`pcr`, ropls for `opls`, mixOmics for `*_da`, …). The cell value mirrors the canonical library's timing for that row and is language-tinted accordingly.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.ref
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_BLAS=OFF, OPENMP=OFF` — pure scalar reference loops, no acceleration. The parity baseline.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.blas
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_BLAS=ON` only — links system BLAS (OpenBLAS), benefits from BLAS thread parallelism.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.omp
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_OPENMP=ON` only — OpenMP in C kernel loops, no BLAS.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.blas+omp
columns.what: libp4a built with both `BLAS=ON` and `OPENMP=ON` — the recommended production config.

columns.label: pls4all.registry
columns.what: `benchmarks.parity_timing.registry.MethodSpec.pls4all_fn` — the canonical pls4all invocation defined by the benchmark suite for each algorithm (algorithm + solver + deflation + …). This is the column used as the parity reference against sklearn / pls / ropls / plsregress.

columns.label: pls4all.python
columns.what: `pls4all._methods.<algo>_fit(ctx, cfg, X, y, …)` — direct FFI binding

columns.label: pls4all.R
columns.what: `pls4all_method(algo, X, y, ...)` — unified dispatcher (33 fits + 24 selectors + 4 diagnostics)
